title: Kanbus clock: metadata-header + parent/issue description layout redesign

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Starting implementation: add live issue/parent snapshot fetch + metadata-header card layouts for created/transition/comment events.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Data-fetch layer complete: each event now resolves live issue metadata via kbs show --json, with parent lookup only when parent exists and ID-prefix/type/status header normalization.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Applying follow-up visual tweaks: parent section fixed to 3 lines and metadata header changed to left-justified with un-abbreviated status (allow right-edge overflow).

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Follow-up tweak applied: metadata header is now left-justified with 3px gaps and full status text (no abbreviation/truncation). Parent section remains 3 lines. tests/test_kanbus_clock.py passes.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Implementing issue-type-based card palettes: task=blue, epic=indigo, story=yellow, bug=red, including comment cards. Header ID/type will be one notch lighter than status.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Issue-type palette update complete for Kanbus cards (including comments): task=blue, epic=indigo, story=yellow, bug=red; ID+type use band-9, status uses band-8, background uses band-2. tests/test_kanbus_clock.py now passes (22 passed).

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Fixing header prefix source bug: derive prefix from issue key/external key or original event issue key, not internal Kanbus ID.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Prefix-source bug fixed in code: header prefix now prefers key/external_id/event issue key before internal kanbus UUID IDs. Added regression tests for BIBS-1234 and key-field cases.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Applying visual tweak: metadata header row gets 1px left padding while preserving current field separation.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Applying header status formatting tweak: remove whitespace from status token (e.g., IN PROGRESS -> INPROGRESS).

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Investigating remaining KANBUS prefix bug: expanding prefix-source detection to include payload/custom key fields and key-pattern extraction before any internal ID fallback.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Prefix resolution hardened: now checks key/external/custom/payload fields plus issue-key pattern extraction before internal IDs. Added regression tests; tests/test_kanbus_clock.py now passes (25). Please verify on device that non-KANBUS project prefixes display correctly.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Applying intensity swap: header ID/type to medium, main lower description block to brightest, parent/top section remains dimmer.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Fixing regression: header prefix must always derive from full issue key (ABC-123 -> ABC), never numeric suffix/short-id values.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Updated prefix rendering for internal kanbus-* IDs: now shows a short handle from the next segment (e.g., kanbus-b31680... -> B31680) instead of KANBUS. Real keys (ABC-123) still render as ABC. tests/test_kanbus_clock.py passes (26).

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Prefix demo: this comment should show B31680 in the header prefix, not KANBUS.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Hardened prefix selection with explicit scoring: full project keys (ABC-1234) always beat numeric short IDs; internal kanbus-* IDs map to a short handle. tests/test_kanbus_clock.py passes (26).

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Prefix verification after hardening: this should show B31680 (or real project key prefix), not 1234 or KANBUS.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Restarted demo check: verify header prefix is not numeric suffix and not KANBUS.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Prefix precedence updated: event.issue_id now wins when it carries a meaningful key/alias, guaranteeing ABC-1234 -> ABC in header even if kbs show returns numeric/internal IDs. Added regression test; tests/test_kanbus_clock.py now passes (27).

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Event-prefix precedence verification: ABC-1234 style keys should now show ABC; internal IDs should never degrade to numeric suffix.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Restarted verification run: prefix should match event key rule.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Second verification comment for prefix display check.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Applied strict event-prefix rule: header prefix uses event.issue_id-derived prefix first, and numeric-only prefixes are rejected. This guarantees ABC-1234 -> ABC and blocks 1234-only prefixes. tests/test_kanbus_clock.py passes (28).

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Prefix verification after strict rule: should never show numeric-only prefix; ABC-1234 style should render ABC.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Restarted verification burst: prefix should use key prefix before dash.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Verification burst 2/2.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Applied strict before-dash prefix rule: _issue_id_prefix_upper no longer uses any after-dash fallback for kanbus-* identifiers. Prefix is always substring before first dash. Updated tests accordingly (28 passing).

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Prefix rule verification after strict before-dash change.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Added payload-prefix override path: if event id is numeric/internal but payload carries a real key (e.g., issue_key=ABC-1234), header prefix now uses ABC. Also disabled kanbus-* event-id override. tests/test_kanbus_clock.py now 30 passing.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Post-fix verification: payload/event key prefix should display before-dash segment only.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Post-fix verification 2/2.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Investigating NO DESCRIPTION on Kanbus clock cards; suspect kbs binary not updated for workspace discovery. Rebuilding/installing kbs, then re-testing via kbs show from workspace root.
